content.text: Initially when I bought a Lenovo M700 Tiny for my homelab, I got a 128GB SSD, so it was okay for some basic services but definitely not enough for my plans (music+images+file server). Luckily about a year ago I bought myself 1TB SATA SSD to triple boot 2 Linuxes + Windows 11 on my PC, as I wanted to daily drive stable distro (Mint), play with less stable, freaky Arch-based OS and keep Windows 11 for games that won’t run on Unix systems due to anticheats. I dropped this idea some time ago and kept 1TB for Windows gaming and 1TB for Linux. I’m definitely not in my gaming prime at the moment so swapping that almost empty SSD was exactly what I needed. I used DiskGenius to migrate all data from SATA SSD (Windows) to my empty NVMe, following this guys tutorial: https://youtu.be/Sdu0bq-Upfw?si=l9wSVE36ojFprxdk

As my homelab supports SATA SSDs I was able to mount it (almost perfectly, unfortunately the tray was missing so I had to make one on a 3D printer, the tray turned out a little tilted but keeps the drive in place). 128GB NVMe drive was left for the system. After everything was connected I had to wipe this drive and initialize it:

I decided to set up samba on Debian 12 as I wanted the most stable experience. I gave this container 8GB of storage, 1 core and 512MB of memory. I mounted the external directory and proceeded with the initial setup:

pct set 100 -mp0 /mnt/pve/ssd-samba,mp=/mnt/dane
apt update && upgrade -y
apt install sudo
adduser sperek
usermod -aG sudo sperek
Then I installed samba and created a password for my user:

apt install samba
sudo smbpasswd -a sperek
I added a basic configuration and restarted this process:

sudo nano /etc/samba/smb.conf

[miniNAS]
   path = /mnt/data
   browseable = yes
   read only = no
   guest ok = no

sudo systemctl restart smbd
From the Proxmox I allowed rwx for everyone:

To connect from my CachyOS system with Nautilus file explorer I had to download gvfs-smb library which allowed me to connect to my service! I made a test file manually which is visible in my container:

sperek@samba:~$ ls -la /mnt/data
total 48
drwxrwxrwx 8 nobody nogroup  4096 Apr  9 09:49 .
drwxr-xr-x 3 root    root     4096 Apr  9 09:29 ..
drwxrwxrwx 2 nobody nogroup  4096 Apr  9 09:23 dump
drwxrwxrwx 2 nobody nogroup  4096 Apr  9 09:23 images
drwxrwxrwx 2 nobody nogroup 16384 Apr  9 09:23 lost+found
drwxrwxrwx 2 nobody nogroup  4096 Apr  9 09:23 private
-rwxr--r-- 1 sperek sperek     26 Apr  9 09:49 siemanko
drwxrwxrwx 2 nobody nogroup  4096 Apr  9 09:23 snippets
drwxrwxrwx 4 nobody nogroup  4096 Apr  9 09:23 template
At the end I configured tailscale connection so that I can connect to my file server from the outside:

sudo apt install curl
curl -fsSL https://tailscale.com/install.sh | sh
sudo tailscale up
Voila! Next plan: self-hosted music player… (^_^)
